title: Kafka Producers Deep Dive
learningObjectives: Tune **acks**, **retries**, and **delivery.timeout.ms** with clear durability semantics
Use **idempotent** producers and explain duplicate handling vs consumer idempotency
Diagnose **metadata** issues and **buffer** exhaustion from producer metrics
Present a coherent **min ISR** + **RF** story for payment-grade topics

sections.title: Why Kafka Producers Deep Dive matters
sections.content:
**Producers** decide **how** events **enter** the **log**: **durability**, **latency**, and **duplicate** behavior **start** here. Senior interviews probe **acks**, **idempotence**, **batching**, and **transactional** trade-offs with **numbers**, not slogans.

Misconfigured **retries** and **ISR** policies cause **duplicate** downstream **effects** or **silent** **durability** gaps—tie fixes to **metrics** and **topic** **policy**.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Kafka Producers Deep Dive
sections.content:
### Day 60 — **Kafka** **producers**

### 1. Durability triangle
**acks**, **retries**, **delivery.timeout.ms**, and **min.insync.replicas** must be reasoned **together**.

### 2. Idempotence
**PID** + **sequence** numbers **dedupe** producer **retries** at the protocol.

### 3. Throughput tuning
**linger.ms**, **batch.size**, **compression**, **buffer.memory**.

### 4. Ordering pitfalls
**in-flight** + **retries** can **reorder** unless constrained.

### 5. Transactions
For **read-process-write** EOS patterns—latency and **operational** complexity rise.

### 6. Story
**Brownout** + **disabled** idempotence → **finance** duplicates; fix **idempotence** + **outbox** consumers.

sections.title: Basic — Producer vocabulary
sections.output:
acks=all waits on ISR policy
enable.idempotence dedupes producer retries
linger.ms trades latency for batching

sections.title: Intermediate — Effective batch size estimate
sections.description: Toy: records per batch from total bytes and avg record size.

sections.title: Advanced — Retry budget toy
sections.description: Cap attempts with a simple retry loop budget.

sections.title: Producer to leader and ISR
sections.description: Producer batches; replicas acknowledge per acks policy.
sections.plantuml:
@startuml
title Day 60 — Producer path
Producer -> Leader : produce request
Leader -> ISRfollowers : replicate
ISRfollowers --> Leader : caught up
Leader --> Producer : ack per acks config
@enduml

sections.title: Exercise — Kafka producers — durability trio (Day 60 assignment)
hints: Name one **metric** and one **config** you would check on-call for this day’s topic.
State a **failure mode** (e.g. rebalance, ISR shrink, schema break) tied to your answer.

sections.title: Use cases — Kafka Producers Deep Dive
sections.content:
## Use cases — **Kafka producers**

### 1) **Payment** **authorization** **events**
**Context:** High-value **records** must survive **broker** **failures** without **duplicate** **charges** on retry.
**Why Day 60:** **acks**, **idempotency**, and **retries** interact with **ISR** policy.
**Implementation outline:**
1. Use **idempotent** producer + **acks=all** with **min.insync.replicas** aligned to **tier**.
2. Set **delivery.timeout.ms** and **retries** so clients **fail** visibly instead of **hanging**.
3. Monitor **record error rate** and **metadata** refresh spikes.
**Failure hook:** **Retry** storm after **NOT_LEADER** misconfiguration.

### 2) **Telemetry** **firehose**
**Context:** Millions of **small** events per second; **cost** sensitive.
**Why Day 60:** **batch.size**, **linger.ms**, and **compression** dominate **efficiency**.
**Implementation outline:**
1. Tune **linger** vs **p99** produce **latency** SLO.
2. Pick **compression** codec with CPU vs **bandwidth** trade-off.
3. Use **null** keys only when **ordering** is irrelevant; watch **sticky** **partitioner** skew.
**Failure hook:** **Buffer** **exhaustion** when **downstream** **backpressure** returns.

### 3) **Outbox** → **Kafka** **bridge**
**Context:** **DB** transaction commits must **match** **published** events.
**Why Day 60:** Producer **ordering** per partition pairs with **outbox** **relay** design.
**Implementation outline:**
1. Single **partition** key per **aggregate** for **ordered** **relay**.
2. **Transactional** producer only if **end-to-end** story includes **consumers** and **sinks**.
3. **DLQ** invalid **payloads** at **schema** boundary.
**Failure hook:** **Partial** publish → **dual** **write** bugs without **idempotent** **consumers**.

### 4) **Mobile** **client** **analytics**
**Context:** **Bursty** traffic from app **sessions**; occasional **massive** spikes.
**Why Day 60:** **max.in.flight** and **ordering** trade-offs when **retries** happen.
**Implementation outline:**
1. Cap **in-flight** when **ordering** must hold with **retries**.
2. **Rate-limit** at **gateway** before **Kafka** to protect **shared** clusters.
**Failure hook:** **Reordering** visible to **real-time** **dashboards** after **retry**.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What does **acks=all** mean?
conceptual.answer: Producer waits for **all** **in-sync replicas** to **ack** according to **broker** **ISR** policy before **success**—pairs with **min.insync.replicas** for **real** durability. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **What does **acks=all** mean?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: Why **enable.idempotence=true**?
conceptual.answer: Producer **dedupes** **retries** with **sequence** numbers so **network** blips **do not** create **distinct** duplicate **records** at the broker **layer**. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **Why **enable.idempotence=true**?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: How do **retries** interact with **ordering**?
conceptual.answer: **max.in.flight.requests.per.connection** >1 with **retries** can **reorder** **batches** on failure—tune for **latency** vs **ordering** risk. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **How do **retries** interact with **ordering**?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: What is **delivery.timeout.ms**?
conceptual.answer: Upper bound for **send** attempts including **retries**—after it **fails** the record **unless** you handle **exceptions**. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **What is **delivery.timeout.ms**?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: **linger.ms** vs **batch.size**?
conceptual.answer: **linger** trades **latency** for **better** **batching**; **batch.size** caps **bytes** per **batch**—both **affect** throughput. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For ****linger.ms** vs **batch.size**?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: Why **compression.type** matters?
conceptual.answer: Less **network** and **disk** at **cost** of **CPU** on **clients** and **brokers**—pick **lz4**/**zstd** per **SLA**. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **Why **compression.type** matters?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: What is **acks=1** risk?
conceptual.answer: Leader **ack** before **followers** **catch** up—broker **loss** may **lose** data if **leader** had **not** **replicated**. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **What is **acks=1** risk?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: **buffer.memory** exhaustion symptom?
conceptual.answer: **block** on **send** or **timeout** depending on **max.block.ms**—indicates **slow** brokers or **too** small **buffer**. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For ****buffer.memory** exhaustion symptom?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: **partitioner** and **sticky** partitioner?
conceptual.answer: **Sticky** batches **same** **partition** for **null** keys improving **batching** efficiency in modern clients. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For ****partitioner** and **sticky** partitioner?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: **Transactional** producer outline?
conceptual.answer: **initTransactions**, **beginTransaction**, **send** + **commitTransaction** coordinates with **consumers** reading **committed** **offsets** with **isolation** **level**—heavier **latency** cost. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For ****Transactional** producer outline?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: Why avoid unbounded **retries** without **jitter**?
conceptual.answer: **Retry** storm amplifies **outage** load on **already** **sick** **cluster**. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **Why avoid unbounded **retries** without **jitter**?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: **metadata.max.age.ms** relevance?
conceptual.answer: How long **client** **caches** **topic** **metadata** before **refresh**—stale **metadata** causes **NOT_LEADER** loops. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For ****metadata.max.age.ms** relevance?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: **enable.idempotence** prerequisites?
conceptual.answer: Typically requires **acks=all** and **retries>0**; **check** **broker** **version** compatibility. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

conceptual.answer: Count of **failed** **sends**—pair with **broker** **under-replicated** **partitions** triage. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**. followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: **Sampling** vs full **trace** for Kafka?
conceptual.answer: At high **RPS**, **sample** **headers** for **correlation** while **metrics** stay **primary** **SLO** view. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. conceptual.question: What does **min.insync.replicas** actually gate for producers using **acks=all**?
conceptual.answer: It requires enough **ISR** replicas to acknowledge before the write is considered **durable** under your policy. If ISR shrinks below **min ISR**, producers with **acks=all** **block or error**, trading availability for **durability**—a deliberate safety lever.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **What does **min.insync.replicas** actually gate for producers using **acks=all**?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: How does **consumer group generation** relate to **rebalances**?
conceptual.answer: Each membership change bumps **generation**. **Stale** members cannot commit offsets; **rebalances** revoke and assign partitions. Frequent generation churn usually points to **timeouts**, **slow polls**, or **unstable** network—not normal steady state.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **How does **consumer group generation** relate to **rebalances**?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: What failure mode shows up first when this is misconfigured under load?
followUps.answer: Typical symptoms: **rebalance storms**, **duplicate delivery** after retries, **metadata refresh loops** (**NOT_LEADER**), or **producer buffer exhaustion**. Tie **// p.put("acks","all"); p.put("enable.idempotence","true");** to observable signals before jumping to code changes.

followUps.question: How would you prove correctness in staging without flaky integration tests?
followUps.answer: Use **deterministic** workloads: fixed keys, controlled partition counts, inject broker pause/leader movement in a test cluster, and assert **offset** progress + **exactly-once** expectations with idempotent sinks or test harnesses.

followUps.answer: Typical symptoms: **rebalance storms**, **duplicate delivery** after retries, **metadata refresh loops** (**NOT_LEADER**), or **producer buffer exhaustion**. Tie **// fencing and epoch for exactly-once producer sessions** to observable signals before jumping to code changes.

followUps.question: How would you prove correctness in staging without flaky integration tests?
followUps.answer: Use **deterministic** workloads: fixed keys, controlled partition counts, inject broker pause/leader movement in a test cluster, and assert **offset** progress + **exactly-once** expectations with idempotent sinks or test harnesses.

codeBased.question: How does `max.block.ms` affect producer `send()` behavior?
codeBased.answer: It bounds how long **send** can block when the **metadata** or **buffer** is unavailable. Too low → **fail fast** under stress; too high → **threads** stall. Tune with **buffer** memory and **delivery** timeout together.

codeBased.question: What is the difference between `enable.auto.commit` and **manual** commits?
codeBased.answer: **Auto commit** commits **periodically** and can commit **before** processing finishes → **at-least-once** hazards. **Manual** commits after processing align **offsets** with **side effects**—with **rebalance** pitfalls if mis-ordered.

seniorScenario.question: **Incident:** **Duplicate** charges after **Kafka** **brownout**.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Freeze risky **consumers**; check **idempotence**.

**(2) Root cause** **Idempotence** **disabled** + **aggressive** **retries**.

**(3) Fix** Enable **idempotence**; **business** **idempotency** keys.

**(4) Prevention** Dashboard **producer** **error** rate. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

followUps.question: How do you communicate blast radius and ETA to stakeholders during the incident?
followUps.answer: For ****Incident:** **Duplicate** charges after **Kafka** **brownout**.**, report **which topics/partitions** lag, **whether writes are still accepted**, **data-loss risk** (ISR), and **consumer backlog time**. Give a **bounded** ETA tied to a concrete mitigation (scale, pause traffic, fail over).

followUps.question: What permanent guardrails prevent a repeat in six months?
followUps.answer: Add **SLOs** on lag and ISR, **game days** for AZ loss and broker restarts, **capacity models** for partitions, and **review gates** for retention/compaction and ACL changes.

seniorScenario.question: **Ops:** **acks=all** but **silent** **loss** risk.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Check **min ISR** vs **RF**.

**(2) Root cause** **min.insync.replicas** too low.

**(3) Fix** Raise **min ISR**; **alert** **under-replicated**.

**(4) Prevention** Policy lint on **topic** configs. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. sections.title: MCQ Quiz — Kafka Producers Deep Dive
sections.description: Thirty questions from basic to advanced — Kafka Producers Deep Dive. Read every option; distractors are plausible but wrong for a precise reason.

questions.question: [Kafka Producers] Which statement best describes **acks=all** in production Kafka/messaging contexts?

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.
options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **acks=all** belongs to the operational model for **Kafka Producers**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [Kafka Producers] Which option is a **plausible distractor** interviewers use about **idempotent producer**?

options.A: A precise, bounded statement tied to ordering, ISR, or consumer groups.
options.B: A blanket claim that ignores partition boundaries or client semantics.
options.C: A metric-backed troubleshooting step.
options.D: A configuration paired with a failure mode.

questions.explanation: Watch for absolutes on **idempotent producer** that ignore **per-partition** semantics and client responsibilities.

questions.question: [Kafka Producers] Incident: user-visible delay worsens after a deploy touching **retries**. What is a credible first hypothesis?

options.A: The deploy changed timing, batching, retries, or resource limits along the critical path.
options.B: The system randomly deleted all state.
options.C: DNS always causes delay; ignore application logic.
options.D: You always need fewer threads when latency grows.

questions.explanation: Tie **retries** to measurable regressions: compare golden signals before/after and narrow to the changed surface.

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.

options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **linger.ms** belongs to the operational model for **Kafka Producers**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [Kafka Producers] You see misconfiguration around **batch.size** in a code review. What is the best first response?

options.A: Map settings to infrastructure policy, then reproduce under load in a controlled environment.
options.B: Delete the topic and recreate it with fewer partitions.
options.C: Set all timeouts to 1ms for safety.
options.D: Disable metrics to reduce noise.

questions.explanation: Validate **batch.size** against defaults, workload, and symptoms (lag, errors, consistency) before changing code.

questions.question: [Kafka Producers] Incident: user-visible delay worsens after a deploy touching **acks=all**. What is a credible first hypothesis?

options.A: The deploy changed timing, batching, retries, or resource limits along the critical path.
options.B: The system randomly deleted all state.
options.C: DNS always causes delay; ignore application logic.
options.D: You always need fewer threads when latency grows.

questions.explanation: Tie **acks=all** to measurable regressions: compare golden signals before/after and narrow to the changed surface.

questions.question: [Kafka Producers] Which statement best describes **idempotent producer** in production Kafka/messaging contexts?

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.
options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **idempotent producer** belongs to the operational model for **Kafka Producers**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [Kafka Producers] Which option is a **plausible distractor** interviewers use about **retries**?

options.A: A precise, bounded statement tied to ordering, ISR, or consumer groups.
options.B: A blanket claim that ignores partition boundaries or client semantics.
options.C: A metric-backed troubleshooting step.
options.D: A configuration paired with a failure mode.

questions.explanation: Watch for absolutes on **retries** that ignore **per-partition** semantics and client responsibilities.

questions.question: [Kafka Producers] You see misconfiguration around **compression** in a code review. What is the best first response?

options.A: Map settings to infrastructure policy, then reproduce under load in a controlled environment.
options.B: Delete the topic and recreate it with fewer partitions.
options.C: Set all timeouts to 1ms for safety.
options.D: Disable metrics to reduce noise.

questions.explanation: Validate **compression** against defaults, workload, and symptoms (lag, errors, consistency) before changing code.

sections.title: Kafka Producers Deep Dive — cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Topic | Rule of thumb | Interview one-liner |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Knob | Effect | Effect |
| acks | Durability vs latency | Durability vs latency |
| idempotence | Retry-safe protocol dedupe | Retry-safe protocol dedupe |
| retries | Recovery vs storm risk | Recovery vs storm risk |
| linger/batch | Throughput vs latency | Throughput vs latency |
| buffer.memory | Backpressure surface | Backpressure surface |
| compression | Bandwidth vs CPU | Bandwidth vs CPU |
| delivery.timeout | Hard cap on attempts | Hard cap on attempts |
| transactional.id | EOS sessions fencing | EOS sessions fencing |
| max.in.flight | Ordering vs throughput | Ordering vs throughput |
| partitioner | Key to partition routing | Key to partition routing |
| Operational metric | Watch lag + ISR + request latency | Say lag per partition and under-replicated partitions |
| Failure drill | Broker bounce + consumer crash chaos | Prove rebalance and recovery SLOs in staging |
| Capacity | Partitions bound parallelism per group | More consumers than partitions stay idle |
| Security | ACLs least privilege per principal | Separate prod cluster or strict topic governance |
